title: Linting for Correctness, Safety, and Maintainability · Gate Defense
summary: Configure a high-signal linter beyond default style warnings. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a risk-based lint policy and fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
Set severity from failure impact and confidence rather than preference strength.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

content.text: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Lint Upgrades, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a risk-based lint policy and fixtures.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Linter upgrades are behavior-free dependency bumps. This fails the Lint Upgrades contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

hints: Name the Lint Upgrades precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Linter upgrades are behavior-free dependency bumps.

stimulus.title: Lint Upgrades failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Recommended Baseline evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Recommended Baseline assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Lint Upgrades applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review new, removed, renamed, and changed rules during tool upgrades.

hints: Name the Lint Upgrades precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates lint correctness. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
